body:
Add a command for administrator, that allows to get logs (in public chat directly for public repositories, and private messages from the bot for private repositories). Logs should be gotten using `$ --status` command, using the session GUID, at the moment this command should work only for `$` command isolations like screen, docker, tmux. We also need to ensure we correctly get information about repository - is it private or public and so on, so we don't expose private data by uploading log to the chat. In both cases we should upload log as a file, that will be response to the message by user containing `/log` command (in private messages we also forward the message containing the session id and also response with /log and also the response from the bot to that message with attached file).

So /log should be done as response to message containing valid session id (that exists at `$ --status`), or we can directly pass `/log <UUID>` in private messages or public chat.

Private logs never go to public chat, no matter where command was triggered.

That command should be accessible only to owners of chats, where bot is available, not everyone can get logs.

We need to collect data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), list of each and all requirements from the issue, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).
title: /log command
